story.title: Dr. Patel's Light Detective Work
story.text:
Hi, I'm Dr. Aisha Patel. I am a scientist at NASA. My job is to find planets that are orbiting distant stars. It’s like being a space detective!

The clues I use are made of light. But the light I see in my telescope is already very old. This is because stars are so far away. The light takes a long, long time to travel to Earth. The Star Distance tells me the Light Travel Time. For some stars, the light is millions of years old. I am looking at the past!

Next, I measure how bright a star looks from here. We call this its Apparent Brightness. But a star's brightness can be tricky. A dim star that is close can look brighter than a super bright star that is far away. I need to know the star’s real brightness, its actual Luminosity.

My computer models use both clues to understand a star. How far is it? How bright is it really? When I know that, I can look for tiny dips in the starlight. Those dips might be a new planet I’ve discovered. My model also has to think about other things, like the star's color and if space dust is blocking my view.

margin_defs.definition: A tool that makes faraway objects look closer.
Computer programs that use rules to show how something works.
Small moments when something goes down or gets lower.

check_prompts: Why is the light from a star old?
What two main clues does Dr. Patel use to understand a star?

vocabulary.definition: The distance that light travels in one whole year.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The closest star to our sun is more than four light years away.

vocabulary.definition: The real, actual amount of light a star gives off.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A giant star has much more luminosity than a small star.

vocabulary.definition: How bright a star looks to us when we see it from Earth.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A nearby candle has a greater apparent brightness than a faraway streetlight.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Patel says she is 'looking at the past' when she looks at stars. What do you think that feels like?
discussion_cards.frame: I think looking at the past would feel ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Patel's model uses distance, brightness, color, and dust. What is another clue you think a scientist could add to their model to learn about a star?
discussion_cards.frame: A scientist could add ___ to their model because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If two stars have the same real luminosity, what must be true about the one that looks dimmer from Earth?
discussion_cards.frame: The star that looks dimmer must be ___.

organizer.title: What Changes a Star's Apparent Brightness?

boxes.label: Cause: A star is very far away.
Cause: A star has very high luminosity.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
